setting.note: Polk Street, block by block, above a dental parlour. Norris pins his people to specific corners of the 1890s city and the flats of the East Bay, most of it erased by the 1906 earthquake a few years after he wrote it. The story then flees south and east, out of the setting entirely, to end in Death Valley.

rights.originalWork: Public domain. Frank Norris, first published 1899; the author died in 1902.
rights.territoryNotes: Public domain in the United States, and in the United Kingdom and the European Union, where the author died more than 70 years ago (1902).
rights.attribution: Frank Norris, McTeague (1899). Text from Project Gutenberg.

palette.accentSourceQuote: gold pieces between the folds of her apron
palette.note: The gold of Trina's hoarded coins, the metal that drives the whole book to its end in the desert. Taken straight from the text and clears WCAG AA on paper.

mcteague.name: McTeague
mcteague.colorSourceQuote: thick, red neck and mane of yellow hair
